page: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. The instance provides 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. No local storage is bundled, but the instance is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 85033.61 and a Geekbench software compilation score of 40274.0. Passmark database operations also perform in the top tier at 21371.02 operations. However, single-core performance is relatively modest, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores falling into the lower tier. Memory bandwidth is stable, registering 1436.97 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 139.87 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Large-block write bandwidth reaches 69.35 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server handles Redis SET operations at 30.96 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 26.42 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds range from 887.85 tokens per second for small 135M models down to 2.93 tokens per second for text generation on 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture offers a high physical core density and dedicated resource allocation without hyperthreading, making it a resource-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures. The lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning, which should be factored into the overall infrastructure design. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, database operations, and scale-out web serving environments that benefit from high physical core counts and a balanced memory-to-core ratio.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v5 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine from the DPSv5 family, powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core and 192.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks demonstrate strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in software compilation and database operations, though single-core performance is more modest. Memory bandwidth is consistent, with uncached reads at 139.87 GB/sec and writes at 69.35 GB/sec. This server is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines that leverage ARM-native architectures.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Ampere Altra physical cores, 192 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance for database and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 48 dedicated physical cores and 192 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine delivering 48 dedicated physical cores and balanced memory for efficient multi-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor
Features 48 dedicated physical cores with no hyperthreading overhead
Equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core)
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations
Premium Storage capable with no local storage bundled
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines
